because I love this space, I love this community & I love what it gives-- & has given-- me. it brings me joy to share my heart, soul & fire on the internet with you all. we are not really strangers.

and this year, I want to bring you along with me. 

I want to give you the opportunity to meet me, and I want to keep things as simple as possible. 

this doesn't mean I won't be using SS et al. 

it means I’ll be continuing to explore & expand how & what I choose to share here.

in 2023, I'm going back to basics. 

my core values: love, peace & integrity. 

my purest expression: truth, courage & liberation. 

my most natural way of being = expansive & unconscionable. 

I'm ready to be me.

the more I think about it, the more that these means of neatly splitting off our communications & expression leave a sour taste in my mouth. I have things to say & do, and they aren't always nicely split off. that's life. that's human. let's be here now.

I trust that those who value my work & presence enough will make the effort to follow, engage with & work with me elsewhere. I've already seen that. I'm already seeing that. & isn't that the REAL point of “building a personal brand”?!

you're getting my thoughts here. you're getting live Rachel, as She happens. it's not the same as me sitting down for an hour to Write Something. I don't want to suppress-- or repress-- myself unnecessarily.
putting monetization aside for a moment, exclusively using another forum for “another kind of content” feels kind of contrived to me.

I want to be able to share my real thoughts, feelings & experiences CLEANLY. 

I want to be able to use this account as a form of catharsis. 
not just my Substack/Medium.

I don't want to write *about* them. 

I want to be able to write about *what's going on for me*, without holding back out of fear that someone may recognise themselves in something that I post.

on visibility & telling my truth as it happens: 

as my audience increases here, there's a good chance that people I know-- people who are actively in my offline/ “personal” life-- will see my tweets.
@LucassRice @ZacharyZork how do you enjoy spending time with people?
when the pics & videos are ready, I’ll see if I can post one of me dancing (topless ⚡). I think you'll understand what I mean. ❤️

my self-consciousness goes away. I am wild & weird & free. my energy is breathtaking. I see it, I feel it, I am it. I come alive in ways that aren't possible in words. I find myself, I see myself, I love myself, I am myself. it's easy. it's peaceful.
and it's true. I love PLAYING in those environments. I love wafting around being steely & unapproachable before morphing into the goofiest livewire of all.
he told me how much it inspired him & I felt myself bloom as he described me. I felt deeply seen by someone who knew “nothing”-- & yet the most important things-- about me.

it’s amazing to notice what's happened & changed. it's amazing to notice what's possible. this morning, one of the photographers came up to me to tell me that watching me dance was pure joy. he said he had never seen someone so effortlessly free.

I'm able to enjoy transience in a way that eluded me when I was younger. I meet incredible people all the time. I love them deeply, dearly. they touch my heart. but I am my own in a way that I wasn't before. it's amazing to notice the difference.

I can dance when (& with whoever) I want. I can talk to whoever I want. I can kiss who I want. I can leave when I want. most of the time, I treasure leaving ALONE. I treasure my own company. I’m not afraid to “wait & see”.

there are few things I enjoy more than going out dancing to a venue or club night I know well without expectations. I’ll see familiar faces, friends & acquaintances, and I am free to make choices without explaining myself.

I think this will change next year. I think I will have a lot more sex. I think I will be a lot more wild & free, and I'm excited for that-- genuinely. I'm excited to be available for that for ME. I’m excited about what that gets to create-- & unlock-- in my life & relationships.

when I don't “owe” you my body, I'm free to make different kinds of decisions. my energy is cleaner. I am less afraid of-- or for-- both of us. I am less anxious. I feel safer & more grounded. I am able to be discerning... & so, I am infinitely more powerful.
... and because not having sex can actually be a really good litmus test for gauging whether or not someone is actually interested in & AVAILABLE FOR YOU in the way that you want... & need.
I do this because it works for me; because it's what I need right now. because I have needed this time & space away from sex (& dating) to be with-- & tend to-- me.
this isn't a puritanical take. during this time, I ran wellness for a popular queer sex party & attended many sex+/conscious sexuality events, inc an intense 2-week tantra training.

I value sex so much that I would rather not have it at all than have it with the wrong person, in the wrong context. I value my body so much that I would rather not have it at all than have it with the wrong person, in the wrong context.
I could be having more sex than I'm having. hell, a part of me WANTS to be having more sex than I'm having. but I don't want--& have never wanted--to have sex for the sake of it, and I’m selective.

I don't want to share intimately here unless I know I am safe. and in practice, nothing is 100% “safe”; safety is an inside job, not sterility. I stretch my capacity for feeling + expressing myself in public, and I do so fiercely & fearlessly. and... I'm doing this for me.

why didn’t I post pictures/videos of myself on this acct until very recently? because THE IDEA MADE ME FEEL SICK. why do I talk day-in, day-out about sex & relationships to/with a male audience & share less of/about myself? bc #boundaries, baby! I have other outlets.
I refuse to drown it it.

even the “lazy” aesthetic is reactionary. even the fluffy hair & nerdiness & natural look. even the stupid fucking naked bed videos. it’s a response to something else. it's a game around something else. this stuff is inescapable for all of us once we look closely enough.

this little girl defied all odds when everyone doubted her. she chose herself when she knew it would be the hardest, most painful & most isolating journey of her life (… at least at first). and she kept choosing herself. I am in awe of her bravery.

https://t.co/CLKjTx6MCl

she is the reason I am who I am. she is the reason I do everything I do.this little girl is the reason I am here.this little girl loved me when I couldn’t love myself. this little girl protected me when I couldn’t protect myself.this little girl knew things she couldn’t explain.

it was—it felt— so lonely. I love her so much, and I feel so sad for her. I feel so sad that in her most formative years, she didn’t get what she needed. I’m also filled with respect & admiration for her.

that little girl, “12 going on 21”, was shut down—physically, mentally & spiritually. she had no space to BE, let alone feel. let alone cry. & no one could see it. & even if or when they could, no one could reach her. no one would meet her.
so much of my early intellectualism was a defence mechanism. I see that now, and I say it with love & gratitude because that defence mechanism was a survival mechanism, and it worked.

I am growing out of needing to explain myself. I am growing out of falling into the trap *of* explaining myself. I am growing out of caring about explanations at all. I want to be here now; I am here now; we are here now. this is all there is. these words are simple & true.

this has been the most painful year of my life to date. and it’s also been the most joyful one. and it’s also been the most peaceful one. by far. and I’m still in it. we’re still in it. it’s hard to explain in words.

you are you, and I am me. we are allowed to disagree, and I am happy for us to be different. I don't want to change you; I want to love you. if you don't want to be loved by me, I can love that too. I don't take it personally. we are just strangers on the internet after all 😉

embracing deep okayness: 

I’m OK with my shadow. I'm OK with my humanity. I’m OK with appearing to be uncompromising & unilateral in my thinking & choosing to alter my approach AFTER-- rather than to prevent-- “being proven wrong”.

learnings: 

- when you aren't automatically on the defensive, you're less likely to be ATTACKED, let alone wounded

- when you give yourself permission to be soft, everyone around you softens too
this is different; this is new. I used to wear my heart on my sleeve & talk a lot about the process of be[com]ing able to forgive myself for one simple reason: it was really hard for me to do so.

I’m proud of myself. I hope you are too.

I committed to being--& becoming-- more congruent, cohesive & truthful. I committed to owning my shit without shame. I grew up... a lot. I continued to let go of living to fulfil someone else’s dream. it hurt. my word was “integration”. I did it, I'm doing it, I live it.

2022 was a year of major transition for me. I resolved not to do anything I didn't really (really!) want to do...which was very new & scary for me. I spoke my truth awkwardly. I got rejected for it. I became stronger, braver & more powerful for it. I fell in love with myself.

I’m not perfect (at all), but I've wanted to operate from as clean an energy field as I could. I have never been a malicious person & I’ve worked hard not to become one. what you see is what you get. &...I invite you to make your own mind up. I want you to trust yourself over me.
I said (almost) nothing to anyone about it for months.

for a long time, I had a chip on my shoulder about being the bigger person. there was no space for me to be. there was no space for me to grieve. this year, I was completely shut down by people I trusted & respected. I responded peacefully-- lovingly, even. inside, I was livid.

I'm acutely aware of what it’s like to be betrayed by people you love & trust. I'm also acutely aware of what it’s like to have people you thought you could lean on not be able to show up for you-- for whatever reasons.

I move so fast that sometimes I lose sight of who & where I used to be. I've grappled with “being a private person” & having things to say, do, share. I've also had to face the pain of my childhood & look at where & how I was shouldering responsibilities that were not my own.

when you answer to yourself, you answer for yourself. 

become your own best advocate.
the more I lean into “doing things that don't make sense”, the happier I am (& the more free I feel)

the upside of operating on trust: 

when you trust yourself, 
you have nothing to fear 

so... 

ofc there's massive upside

I want to show that it's possible to do things differently. 

I want to show that it's possible to live differently. 

without shame, without fear, without self-hatred & with the fiercest + most devoted love for life & humanity imaginable.

bigger picture:it makes me sad to see how we (as a collective) have cheapened & degraded sexuality & sensuality. 

when I say this,what I'm referring to is the loss of meaning & loss of connection. 

our bodies are our homes. 

there’s nothing “dirty” or “impure” about them.

I don't take how fortunate I am for granted. 

the only person I have to justify my decisions to is me. clearly, that's huge. and... you're only young once. 

I've lived many lives to get here. 
I'm not selling out now.

I have no plans to make that kind of content for one simple reason: it doesn't feel in integrity for me. 

the question that's emanating for me = “does this excite me? or, better yet, does it make my heart sing?” 

taking pictures/videos of myself for $ doesn't spark joy for me.

a few people have asked me whether I’d considered making an OF. their default reaction to me saying I wanted to integrate erotic content into my public presence here was to assume I was going to make & share “adult” pictures & videos. I found that interesting.

safety will not be not created from insecure foundations. we can't become congruent when we aren't allowed (or allowing ourselves) to be. this is why deep change is always deeply personal. everything else is an inevitable ripple effect.

we compartmentalize when we feel unsafe. 

compartmentalizing is an effective survival mechanism. it's also (invariably) a blunt instrument. 

why? 

it doesn't teach us a more valuable tool: discernment. 

instead, it teaches us [how] to shut ourselves & other people down.

compartmentalization is the enemy of congruence. 

when we’re taught to hide aspects of who we are (or what we get up to on the weekends), we’re also taught to be ashamed of them... 

& never to stop to question, “wait a sec. ashamed of what? & why? is this really mine to own?”

CLARIFICATION POINTS 

-  on my end: this is not a sales pitch

LOGISTICS 

HOW: asynchronous comms, via a secure, end-to-end messaging app of our choice. 

I AM NOT HERE TO DOX YOU: I don't need to know your identity to take part in this with you; happy to chat via Wire w/ anon Twitter accounts (reach out via DM)

I know this is a sensitive topic & your story provides me with invaluable insight that I’m deeply grateful for. https://t.co/4QHjSqVwj2

HOW I WANT TO USE THIS INFORMATION 

with your permission,

I would like to share screenshots of our conversation anonymously on social media eg Twitter 

(with any personal/identifying info retracted) 

to facilitate deeper, more open & more nuanced conversations on this topic

I'm not a cop or a lawyer. 

you were accused of being a predator OR behaving in a way that was experienced by others as being “inappropriate”. 

- what did that feel like for you? 
- how did that impact you? 
- who were you then?
- how are you now? 

THAT’S what interests me.

WHERE I’M COMING FROM 

I’m interested in having a frank conversation with you about your feelings & experiences as it relates to an experience that most men are terrified of & can't/won't talk about for myriad valid reasons.

I didn't realise how much I would enjoy this work. 

but now, it makes perfect sense. 

it's the natural integration of Everything Else. 

it's deeply complementary to everything that came before. 

& it's impossible for me to be scared of men. 

humbled, grateful, surrendered ;) https://t.co/yXzMX9GNrA

everyone is different. 

everyone has different wants (+ needs!). 

the best doms make & break their own rules. 

they create containers that feel like sanctuaries. 

they follow what's alive
& only go as fast as they can feel.

I see its remarkably effortless healing potential... not just for individuals. 

for RELATIONSHIPS. 
for communities. 
for ecosystems. 

I feel an expansion of my own capacity. 
I feel a deeper sense of trust & understanding.

great power is a great responsibility. 

that level of power (& ownership) is terrifying. 

I wasn't ready for it before. I didn't want it. so no one was going to hand it to me. 

but I'm different now.... & that excites me.

it isn't even (necessarily) sexual. 

it's about safety.
it's about the power of surrender. 

the power of letting yourself be met, known + loved in & for all that you are. 

nowhere does that become more transformative than in the realms of what's private, hidden & taboo.

it's a relief to know that the OP isn't going to drop you. 

that the OP has got you. 
that you can REST. 

it's an experience like nothing else.

it's a relief to be able to test the sanctity of the container. 

it's a relief to be seen through. 
it's a relief to be held. 

it's a relief to know that you CAN be held.

it's a relief to not have to think. 

it's a relief to not have to try. 

it's a relief to have someone else know what they want of, with & from you. 

it's a relief to have someone else tell YOU what to do.

it's a relief to be able to listen.

all power is RELATIONAL. 
without relationships, there is no power. 
there is only grasping for. 

we all want to be felt. 
we all want to be known. 

it's human
& it's beautiful.

of course, "powerful men" want to submit. 
of course, "powerful men" want to surrender. 
of course, "powerful men" want to serve.

because that is what power ACTUALLY is. 

FREEDOM TO.
not freedom from. 

... which includes the freedom to "fail" & "look stupid", of course ;)

I desperately wanted to switch my brain off. 
I desperately wanted to be taken care of. 
I didn't have space for anything else. 

we are but mirrors.

I was hyper-sub for a very long time. 

I didn't understand dominance. 
its power. its pleasure. 
what it creates, in & of itself. 

I didn't want to. I wasn't able to. 
because I needed it myself.

TAKE OWNERSHIP by speaking from a place of “I”. 

- what do you want? 
- what do you need? 

when it comes to communication & managing expectations. then, SHARE THIS with the OP. 

clear is kind. unclear is unkind
... to you AND them 

(with thanks to @BreneBrown) https://t.co/8YH8IDh8jT

most importantly: find a way of saying/doing this in a way that makes you feel confident, grounded & like YOU’VE come out on top. 

⚡️prefer voice notes to texting? send a vn

⚡️prefer calling to texting? call her. if she doesn't pick up, send a text or send a voice note

simply... be factual. 

if it makes you not want to make the effort to reschedule, say that. 

if it makes you feel like you're not on the same page about what you're looking for & value in a relationship, say that. 

if it makes you feel taken for granted, say that.

⚡️THE CRUCIAL INGREDIENT ⚡️

be honest with them about how THEIR BEHAVIOUR affects 

- you
- your behaviour
- YOUR INTEREST IN THEM

without threatening or criticising them

second question: how do you want the OP to treat you? 

if you want them to respect you, tell them their behaviour made you feel disrespected. 

then, follow up by saying what you want to BE respected (eg your time) & how you felt about it (hurt, frustrated, disappointed, etc).

first question to ask: what do you want here? 

if you're not that interested in the OP anyway, cut your losses. 

if you ARE (or were... or just want to try this out to see what happens), read on ⚡️

you don't have to be a dick to women to refuse to let them disrespect you. 

in fact... 

you will be so much MORE effective at *actually* getting them to respect you if you can be the bigger person in situations like this.

let’s be real: 

you were really looking forward to that date! you made the effort to plan it! & she just disrespected that! 

she just disrespected YOU. 

THAT ISN’T COOL. 

it makes sense that you feel hurt & disappointed! 

THAT IS A LEGITIMATE RESPONSE.

why: if you don't speak up for yourself in the moments that matter, you are denying that your experience matters. 

... and that’s even more painful than acknowledging that the OP’s behaviour made you doubt them/yourself.

what do I mean by speaking unarguably? 

if you're a man who's used to feeling shut down by women, I think you already know 😉 

I mean... 

speaking in a way that is almost impossible for the OP to twist. 

how? speaking YOUR truth, FOR you. what's ACTUALLY going on here?

YOUR PEACE IS YOUR POWER. 

and... your feelings are powerful too. 

you are ALLOWED to talk about what's real. you are ALLOWED to talk about how someone else’s behaviour has affected you. 

you will be most EFFECTIVE when you do so unarguably.

remember... 

they've already rejected you.

which also means that in practice, there's already (some) evidence that this won't go anywhere/isn't a good use of your time & energy. 

so, there's also no rush.

what this also means = TAKING YOUR POWER BACK

❌ don't play games (unless you want the OP to play games w you)

❌ don't toy with them (unless you want the OP to toy w you)

✔️ do wait until you feel calm, clear & grounded to respond

onto practicalities.. 

the #1 weapon in your arsenal is not reacting emotionally to the OP. 

this doesn't mean not feeling your feelings. 

what this does mean is processing them away from the OP (who has already indicated that they may not be able to meet you emotionally).

key points:

if you choose to do this, you are doing it FOR YOU, not them. 

this is a power move. 

and, it's a power move FOR YOU.

not a power move over them.

my challenge to you: this weekend, take a look at your relationships.

“communication problems” are really about power (99% of the time: unexpressed feelings & desires).

the same is true of “intimacy problems”.
power differentials are (or can be) very useful; when leveraged correctly, they make people feel safe & secure with one another

this is what I mean when I talk about:

1) power being relational
2) power differentials NOT being inherently harmful or oppressive-- quite the opposite
you have chosen this. they're cracking the whip. you're begging for more... and holding your breath. in a GOOD way ⚡️

with informed, enthusiastic consent, being a sub-- in all its flavours & (dare I say) extremes-- can feel so good, ESPECIALLY if the OP is at some level exploiting their power by humiliating or degrading you
being a loving dominatrix to the people you serve, in a mutually consenting relationship, is MUTUALLY PLEASURABLE & fulfilling

all business is a form of kink play.

when I say, "pay me (& trust me with what happens next),” that’s a dom move.

when you say, "here's my money, help me (I trust you with what happens next),” that’s a sub move.

in that exchange = EROTIC tension (ET). 

ET = generative af

you are not giving your power away by asking for a meeting or asking for money (investment) for your startup

you are giving your power away in the way you’re showing up & the energy you’re giving off

it doesn't feel good for you, it doesn't feel good for them

switch things up!

ex: you're a founder & you've spent all week trying to get potential investors to reply to your emails, to no avail 

go back to the drawing board & ask yourself one question:

“where am I giving my power away?”
we feel bad when “things are going badly” not because “things are going badly” but because some part of us is fucking-- & being dishonest-- with ourselves

everything in life is a feedback loop. trust the signals. fuck the noise. double-down on what's working by doubling down on what feels good. if it feels bad, flip the coin. ask yourself “what does this tell me about what I really want?”
when you're able to not only take credit for that, but OWNERSHIP, you've done it; indisputably, you’ve already won
the real prize for being a revolutionary is living in a way that has never, ever, ever been done before... and is on your own terms

Women are taught to give their power away to men.

This is a story about how women can reclaim their power with men.

We teach with the clarity of our example.

This example wouldn't exist without him.

#100greatmen
•

I was rooted in my body.

There was nothing to take advantage of.

I was a fuck no.

And that was that.

I made it clear that he needed to respect me.

→ He was safe to express himself
→ He was safe to express what he wanted in explicit sexual terms
→ He was safe to express what wanted to do WITH ME in explicit sexual terms

But there was no option whatsoever of acting on it.
And I held by own not by ripping his face off for DARING TO EXPRESS HIS DESIRE TO FUCK ME (GROSS PERVERT), but saying no clearly & emphatically.

Here's the alchemy of this interaction:

I managed to state-shift.

In one of the most intense situations I've ever been in, I held my own.

I ask for a massage. 

Shoulders & back, only.

I get my massage.

It's surprisingly good.

He asks me what I want instead.

I take a while to think about it.

To tune into what I feel comfortable with.

To get out of "get the fuck away from me", and into...

"Hmm, what could be nice for me?"

He says he can "fuck me closer to God".

Somehow, I maintain my composure.

I smile and say, "that sounds great, but no."

It did sound great. Just not with him.

He's double my age, easily. Probably more.

A respected tantra teacher, facilitating retreats globally.

We have 20 minutes together.

His presence feels slimy to me.

I don't want him anywhere near me.

But I'm determined not to shrink.

There's a man in front of me. He repulses me.

He's repulsed me since I met him.

There's something about him that just feels...

Snaky. Conniving. Wolfish.

What I like to call...

"Big Taker Energy"

He reminds me of other men I've known.

We're onto the more intimate rituals now. 

The kinds of rituals where anything (yes, anything) can happen. 

And it's the ultimate test of my sovereignty & bodily autonomy.

I know why I'm here. I know what I came here to do.

And it isn't "free love" or "join a nudist commune".
And right now, I want to jump out of my skin.

Imagine, if you can...

A circle of semi-naked people. Paired up in twos.

Every few minutes, the outer circle moves two spaces to the left.

A new partner. A new opportunity for connection.

I am in this circle.
I am one of these people.

I chose to be here.

I love Truth. 

And sometimes I hate what it creates. 
Or rather, I hate how it's manipulated.

Here is a story that triggered & surprised me.

I share it with you because few things are what they seem.

I can feel my rage just writing this.

I love what this story represents. 

And I hate what it could be said to insinuate.

"He would only be a predator if I allowed myself to be prey."

So begins Day 6 of #100greatmen.

And a story that's been sitting in my Medium drafts for a couple of months.

But first...

And I give the most fucks for this because I've earned it. I deserve it. I give myself the right to claim that.

I would give every single fuck I have for these things.

What do you give a fuck about?

I give a fuck about making the world a better place, but only as a byproduct of all of the above.

Because I give the most fucks about living a wildly expansive, joy-filled & FREE life. I give the most fucks about being HAPPY in myself & my own skin.

→ I give a fuck about love & relationships

→ I give a fuck about systemic injustice

→ I give a fuck about changing the face, nature, texture, quality & EXPERIENCE of power

→ I give a fuck about changing the way we think, talk & feel about privilege

What I give a fuck about:

Equipping people with the skills they need to break out of survival mode & THRIVE with a passion, fervour & commitment that defies reason (AKA, everything that came before).

Because it gets easier when you let yourself admit how much you care. 

And it gets even easier when you stop being afraid to say that. 

Now, I love saying it. It strengthens my resolve.

In case it wasn't already clear, I give a fuck about the things I talk about.

In fact, I give lots of fucks.

And because of that, I have nothing to lose. 

I can only win. I've already won.

My $0.02 on sharing your work/art/heart/soul with the world: 

It gets easier the more you do it. And not because you care less.

It gets easier when you let yourself admit how much you care. 

When you give what you care about LEGITIMACY. 

To yourself, first & foremost.

How I like to create:

From the heart. With soul. With fire. With substance.

I like to spill my heart onto the page.

→ I like to post unedited
→ I like to post unfiltered

But I also write a lot more than I share here. And I've been sharing my work publicly for 10+ years.

Litmus test:

1. "Am I doing this because I want to do it or because I feel I should do it?"

2. "Where am I not giving myself permission to listen to, trust & take care of myself first?"

When I notice I'm doing something I don't actually want to do, I stop doing it.

I get better at this every single day.

In a nutshell:

→ I post what I want, when I want, how I want [to]

→ I only like/rt when I want to

→ I only comment when I want to

→ I only send messages when I want to

My social media strategy:

"FUCK THE SHOULDS"

We don’t.

When we see with our real eyes, we realise what was already here.

When we refuse to shame ourselves, we don't just open our hearts to love. 

We open ourselves to being & becoming love. 

We become more loving simply by embracing what we already are.

This happens naturally as our true nature is revealed.
The antidote to shame is unconditional [self-]love.

Because if something was inherently wrong with us, why would WE want to connect with that? 

Of course, we’d want to deny it (ourselves) at all costs. 

It makes sense.

Perfectionism relies on shame to survive. 

And shame is what disconnects us from ourselves. From who we really are, ie love.

I feel its wrath whenever I “make a mistake” or “don't say something quite right”. I feel it in the pit of my stomach. 

Ugly. You're ugly. You're disgusting. 

BLEURGH.

There is no point to this story. There is no end & no beginning. There is no hero's journey arc. This is just a point in time. (Or at least, that's the story I'm telling myself.)

Here I am. There you are. Here we are.

Where are you?

I feel most alive in words. I feel most myself in words. 

As I write, alone. 
As I spill onto the page. 

But words are not (& will never be) all of me. The juxtaposition. There is so much that is beyond words. 

And when I take my own identity out of it, it all makes sense.

The perils of (mis)interpretation. We are expert meaning-makers. I want this account to be a torrent of poetry. A space of love & beingness. The embodiment of what I care about. A place of feelings & felt connection.

And, as I write these words, I feel it. 

I feel myself.

It's hard to describe in words. I've written so much about it over the years that I forget that you don't know me. I forget that you can't see inside my head. I see & feel how we're all just Little Human Beings jostling for space, time, presence & attention and it humbles me.

~ Saturday Stories~

I'm still learning self-compassion, and how much of myself it's safe to share here. How much space it's safe to occupy. I've always existed at the intersection of multiple worlds.

Are you sorry for their loss? Or are you sorry to hear about it?

ON LOSS

I'm not doing it anymore.

@PerfectGuide_ Learn when & how to trust yourself. THAT’S power.

I like to imagine all the worst things that could be thrown at me. 

Insults, slander, whatever. 

I like to watch myself stand in it & say-- “hey, maybe that's true. You know what, that's PROBABLY true.” And laugh about it. And watch myself soften.
One of my favourite exercises is “I'm a fucking awful piece of shit”.

People who trust themselves are trustworthy not because they never “fuck up” in the eyes of the world or another (many others) but because they are far more likely to have the courage to ADMIT it. 

We cannot change what we do not allow ourselves to acknowledge & OWN.

What will you lose by letting go of shame? What are you scared to lose, even?
How & where has shame protected you?

We face our demons by building capacity for them. By learning to hold ourselves. By learning to love ourselves with them. 

Then, and only then, can we let them go. 

We will miss them. They have served a helpful purpose. 

How & where does shame serve you?

When you don't have to face [up to] your own personal responsibility, you’re also protected from pain-- to at least some degree. 

At the expense of life. 
At the expense of joy. 
At the expense of aliveness.

And, all these things can be deeply confronting.

From personal experience: 

There is comfort (& safety) in fawning (& victimhood) too.

Fawning isn't just a trauma response. 

It's a trauma response that's rooted in-- and relies on-- shame. 

That's why it's so persistent. And pervasive. 

We fawn for the same reason that we victimise ourselves: we believe it's who & what we are.
We fawn when we want to be loved & think we can't as we are.
We fawn when we want to belong & think we can't as we are.

I am many, many, many things. 

But I will not pretend to be something or someone that I am not.

But I know our flaws. I know our humanity. 

I know that all the coaches, the leaders, the “paragons of wisdom” that you see are just as fucked up, insecure, neurotic & emotionally unstable as you & me. Sometimes, much more so.
And I wish I could say I was angry. I wish I could rage against the machine.
And, I think about everyone that I’ve lost. I feel the weight of grief. I know what it’s like to have friends & mentors exit your life suddenly, with no explanation.

I adore my energy & relentlessness. 
I am in love with who I am & what I stand for. I am proud of who I am becoming.
I think about how much time, energy & money I have poured into working on myself. I think about my commitment to cutting through my BS.
I think about a text I received from my parents yesterday, asking me what I had achieved this year.
I think about the use of the phrase “beyond my years” when I know for a fact that I have experienced things in my life that the vast majority never will.

I think about all the people who paid lip service to their so-called appreciation of my “depth & wisdom beyond my years”, but-- when it showed them things about themselves they didn't want to see-- shut me out.
I think about the ways I've subjugated myself to survive.
I think about how my lived experiences have persistently, subtly-and-not-so-subtly, been discredited.

Sometimes, I think about all the ways I've been wronged. 

Often brutally.

I then went into a meeting with 3 people who said they wanted to listen to me but then did everything in their power not to.
I was not significantly underweight for my age & height (my BMI was above 16 & I was 14 yo).
On the day I was sectioned, I ate 4 boxes of Rice Krispies, 2 cups of milk & a cup of orange juice for breakfast.
When I was sectioned, I was none of those things. I was also a child.

For anyone who isn't aware:the Mental Health Act is supposed to be used only in exceptional circumstances when the person concerned = at immediate high risk to themselves or others & refusing treatment.

In 2012, I was placed on a 6-month Section 3 off the bat for refusing to return to the hospital I had been a patient at for the last 8 months. 

I had been going from unit to unit pretty much nonstop for 2 years.

People being worried about me has often felt unsafe. 

More about their judgements (their needs, their feelings) than my own. 

And it quite literally led to me being unlawfully locked up against my will.

Things I don’t want you to know about me: 

When you ask me if I'm OK, I experience shame. 

- I feel as if you're judging me. 
- I feel my body contract. 
- I feel unsafe.

A few years ago, I didn't believe that I could or would be in this position. 

I didn't know my own strength. I didn't realise how capable I was. 

It's not hyperbole to say that my world has changed beyond my wildest dreams, but sometimes words seem so trite.

This story is just one of many - part of the rich tapestry of experiences that have shaped me. 

I am proud of who I am becoming and appreciative of all that I have learned along the way. 

I know that I am resilient. And that makes me feel unstoppable.

I'm sharing this because it's so easy to 'other' people who aren't like us... or to judge before we understand. 

With the benefit of hindsight (and a good few years under the bridge), I am incredibly grateful for my experiences.

What did I need then? 

In simple terms, I needed someone to believe in me. 
Or, more accurately, I needed to *know* that someone did. 

Stuck in hospital with few prospects, it's no wonder that my fellow 'inmates' & I felt hopeless. Anyone in that situation would.

I've always had a distaste for the 'beautiful tragedy' narrative, so gritted my teeth and did the work. Somehow, I got myself out of a very, very dark hole. 

But it wasn't 'somehow'. It took a lot. It took everything I had. I had it within me, and it was still hard.

"With great power comes great responsibility."

I was a vulnerable teenager with little agency over her own life. But this isn't about me. This is about how we respond to pain & suffering (within ourselves & others) + how we learn (and teach others) to be resilient.

Words are powerful. 

At the age of 15, after spending 3 years inpatient in psych care, I was labelled 'chronic' and told that I would likely be a so-called 'revolving-door patient'. 

Thankfully, this isn't (wasn't) true. But the ethical issues remain.

A good life is one in which we are no longer afraid of what hurts or what matters. 

Some closing thoughts for 2018.

A good life is defined by its sense of possibility. It's yours to have, hold and cherish, requiring only honesty. A good life is painfully, powerfully connected, its essence as electric as it is intangible. It can be whatever you want it to be, but living it takes courage.

A good life is imperfect. It's good enough, and that's enough: it doesn't need to pretend to be anything that it isn't. It doesn't need to push itself beyond its limits. Instead, it accepts the reality of its difficulties and makes something beautiful out of them.

What does it mean to lead a good life? 

Much like human beings, a good life is defined not by its circumstances, but its character. 

A good life is a full life, abundant with love and laughter.

A good life is a visceral one: it forces you to feel.
